text:
Shady, shady, shady. They told me in order to get an upgrade (mind you, I haven't upgraded in over 3 years), i need to upgrade my plan - a $24 increase per month. Without doing this, I have to pay $490 for a new phone up front and the rest gets billed monthly. I upgrade my plan,  to then see that now I have to pay $700 upfront and I'm told that's a great deal and perk by tmobile. I have been with them for over 17 years and can do much better with Verizon,  AT&T or spectrum mobile. They didn't want to let me return my plan to what it was before, but was able to finally get them to. I guess tmobile is hurting after that huge law suit they have to pay everybody out over the data beach so to make up for it, they give you misleading information, catchy buzz words and lies to con you into spending evergreen more money on their awful product. 

Will never trust them again and I'm leaving them after 17 years. (Also now that I'm looking at other carriers, I see average rates for my $100 plan with tmobile ranges anywhere from $50-$80 with other carriers. Wish I left these shady people sooner.

"I have had the absolute worst experience with T-Mobile. Due to their negligence and lack of competent customer service, they wrongfully sent my account to collections! Despite making all payments on time and having no prior issues, they somehow managed to mismanage my account information, leading to unnecessary and undeserved damage to my credit score.

When I reached out to their customer service to rectify their mistake, I was met with endless excuses, confusion, and no clear path to a resolution. Instead of taking accountability, their representatives passed me around from department to department with no one willing to fix their mistake. It’s evident that customer care is NOT a priority for T-Mobile.

Their incompetence in handling this situation has caused me stress, wasted my time, and left me to deal with the unnecessary burden of clearing my name with the collections agency. I trusted T-Mobile with my service and they completely let me down. If you value your credit and your peace of mind, stay far away from T-Mobile!"

edits.text: Absolutely a terrible company.  Bad service.  Bad connection.  Bad security. Everything Bad. Verizon is expensive.  But atleast they care for quality,  protection against data breaches. T-mobile Absolutely doesn't care about you as the consumer.

I used to be a T-MObile customer and switched to Verizon. Reason when they had the breach in August of last year my SSN, EIN, was stolen during the breach. And T-mobile never even contacted me to let me know it was stolen. Until this day they have not told me. I found out through my credit apps. Had to lock my credit. And pay extra money a month to try and protect myself.  My phone number is currently being used in a scam. I kept the number I had with t mobile. And have the same with with Verizon. I get calls all the time and voicemails from ppl saying they sent me money through a website to purchase products I don't sell or have anything to do with. T-mobile lossed all my information and has done nothing. Absolutely nothing.

THEY ARE KNOWINGLY & WILLINGLY RUNNING A SCAM TO GET YOUR MONEY, THEY ARE LYING TELLING YOU THAT YOU PAY NOTHING FOR 6MOS FOR INTERNET SERVICES THEN ON THE 7TH MONTH ITS $30MO. OR $25MO. W/ AUTO PAY HOWEVER IN ONLY 21 DAYS THEY ARE CHARGING YOU $6OMO AND AFTER I CALLED CORPORATE THEY HAVE AN OH WELL NONCHALANT ATTITUDE AND THIS IS HOW MUCH IT REALLY IS AND YOU CAN TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT AND WENT AS FAR AS TO SAY THAT NOTHING WILL HAPPEN TO THEM REGARDING THE FALSE ADVERTISING, BREACH OF CONTRACT, MISLEADING & THEFT BY DECEPTION BECAUSE THEY'VE BEEN THROUGH THIS TOO MANY TIMES & NO ATTORNEY IS BRAVE OR POWERFUL ENOUGH NO GO UP AGAINST THEM & ALTOGETHER I PUT IT ON MY LIFE BEGGING THE DIFFER SHE SAID I WOULDN'T BECAUSE IT IS WHAT IT IS BUT I ASSURED HER THAT ITS A NEW DAY FOR THEM & THANK GOD I RECORDED EVERYTHING & A LAWSUIT IS ENSUE‼️
If you are not connected to wifi it is terrible. Unlimited internet lol if it even loads.

Either there is no one working for T-Mobile that knows the promotions or they are all liars. I recently upgraded to the iPhone 16 I was told I didn’t qualify for the free upgrade to the 16 pro in store then when I called they told me I did qualify and didn’t understand why I was told in store that I didn’t. Long story short I was told that my bill would definitely stay the same and wouldn’t change at all. That was a lie along with a credit that was issued it just disappeared along with my trade in amount for my old phone. I’ve been with them for 23 plus years I’ve always had several lines but with my recent experience I will be changing companies completely after the current phone that was supposed to be free is paid off. 
If there is a lawsuit against this issue with them I’d like to join in what they are doing to customers is not legal at all. PLEASE DONT USE T-MOBILE.

My outcome after writing this review. Received a call from T-Mobile HQ. They were very courteous and worked everything out with me. I stayed with the company. Happy with their service! I now have a direct contact at HQ if slaes people try and BS me.

Complaint:
I made a formal complaint with FTC and BBB as well. Here's my exeprince I had with the T-Mobile Store Salesperson, initials "S.L". And, the customer service supervisor when I called their support number. To be blunt, both lied to me about what they were going to provide. I specifically told "S.L." what I wanted. I wanted to pay all fees and costs for the new cell phone. The phone also has a $800.00 promotion for trade in (upgrade). At that time I paid $483.44 on 06/28/24. Then in July, I get a bill for $140.55 that included a past due amount from the service from my old phone, $80 for plan, $39.38 equipment charge and a $7.00 Late Fee! They had my bank account number and also I paid the $483.44!!! They nickled and dimed me. Called "S.L." and asked him why...his answer was I told you the first month was going to be more. Which was BS! He thinks he told me...I am very detailed and believe me, he did not. He only saw BIG COMMISSION CHECK! Anyway, I didn't get anywhere with him except to be blown off. Then a month later, I call customer service and explained the issue I was having. A supervisor said she would look into it and call me back. Which she did. She told me if I the balance of $283.26 for the cell phone, my bill would be $75/month starting in October 2024. She also provided me a credit of $100.00 so I paid the remaining $183.26 on my company credit card. I thanked her for her assistance and praised her for her efforts. She stated I will have to pay a balance of $50 in September and the $75/month would begin in October. Surprise! October comes and my monthly bill is $80.38/month. This is what they expect me to pay for the remainder to pay off the new cell phone. T-Mobile calls it a "FINANCE CHARGE". In my opinion...it's contract A CONTRACT! In their advertisement, they say NO CONTRACT. Which is TOTAL BS!!! Instead, they make you a captive to thier company by providing MONTHLY CREDIT for 2 years as long as you don't default. On October 16, 2024, I go to the CORPORATE store located in the Westfield Countryside Mall in Clearwater, FL. to get this resolved. Salesman "H", assisted me and did find that there was a "mis-communication" on how "S.L." and the customer service supervisor explained why this was happening. I will say "H" showed empathy and apologized for the "other" 2 T-Mobile co-workers. I found out I owe another $650.00 on the new cell phone...after paying the initial $350.00, $183.28 (the $283.28 to pay off the phone with $100 credit) and the $800 I was to receive when I switched to new plan with the new phone. So the outcome is...I can pay off the $650 and buy the phone outright or have another carrier pay for it when I change carriers. He said he would provide my complaint to the manager who comes back on Friday. We shall see if he/she will call me and see how to retain me (even though I am a "captive") and provide a resolution. After 15 years with Sprint/T-Mobile, this is their loyalty program. I HIGHLY DO NOT RECOMMEND T-Mobile. If you do, pressure the salesperson to write everything down in detail. Document every little detail rather than listen to the BS. Read the FINE PRINT. The salesman will BS you for thier commission! Don't let them talk you into signing up. Look on YouTube, a former employee tells the DECEPTIVE SALES PRACTICES of T-Mobile. Lastly, when I visited the store today, a lady stated (in front of the salesman), they did the same thing to my father...told him the same thing. You should ahve seen the face on the salesman! He wanted to crawl under a rock...
Great service and customer support. I wouldn’t want another carrier.

Stay away they are absolutely horrible. They convinced me us to leave ATT with the promise of free phones and here we are paying for them. They even went back and listened to the recording and said sorry you are misinformed!! Run away it’s not worth it! Was in the phone 4 hours to get to that nothing resolved except the person they trained and listened to on the recording gave the wrong information. He even called us back the next day to close the deal. But they refuse to do anything.

BUYER BEWARE!! The location at Meadowood Mall in Reno, Nevada is a third-party contracted locale. And they have NO IDEA how T-Mobile works. We just left T-Mobile after 15 years as our carrier because of their incompetence, and due to every customer service rep, retail clerk, tech support, manager, heck, you name it, we've spoken with for the last year, who refused to repair the problem created by the creatures at this store. No one wanted to fix the clearly erroneous handling of our new phones and watches by the idiot who sold them to us. We were passed around, placated, and eventually they all went silent.
Our little over $300 month bill jumped to almost $600, simply because the person didn't know how to input information, and no one would fix it afterward. We went to three (3) different T-Mobiles in our town, and nothing. Have spent hours on the phone with these cretins. Nothing. I even emailed a manager at the location on Keystone Avenue, AFTER he asked me to. (A little bit after he told me to, to be clear.) Still. Nothing. 
Stay away from T-mobile. They don't care about you, until you leave. Then you get a 'sad face' from the guy unlocking your phones, and 'why are you leaving?'. Um, read our notes. They are 10 times more lengthy and detailed than this review. Literally cried in two of their stores because of how we were treated. Go to Spectrum, Verizon, heck, get a burner phone. Do anything but business with these people. They want your money, and care less about your loyalty.

Keep and switch is a scam. I was told by the representative when I purchased the phone that I was eligible for a promo credit. After completing the application I was denied because the information was entered incorrectly. The rep said she would reenter and to check my status again in 30 days.  Denied again.  Called customer service and they told me that it was denied due to a duplicate that said it had already been awarded. It was escalated to another team to fix and I’m still waiting. I submitted the original request on 8/15. It’s now 10/15 and TMobile can’t seem to fix this issue that is with their own system. Their customer service team just consistently apologizes but they haven’t been able to do anything. I will continue to call until this is resolved but rest assured I won’t be telling anyone to use TMobile. $750 later.  Maybe I’ll eventually get my $$$. Think twice before switching.
TRASH MOBILE..

"After being a devoted T-Mobile customer for over a decade, the recent plunge in their customer service quality has been downright appalling. Each interaction with their support team has been a nightmare—issues are ignored, and the so-called 'assistance' is useless. It's like the company has forgotten that customers are the backbone of their business. Their promises of efficient support are nothing but empty words. I'm beyond disappointed and utterly frustrated. T-Mobile, you need to remember what customer service actually means. Shame on you."

Hope this does the trick!

Multiple salespeople tell you that the first two weeks are a free trial period and that you won't be charged anything if you cancel within this period. That proved untrue.
Since the access speed was extremely slow, I had to cancel it within a week of activation, and the rep confirmed that I wouldn't be charged anything. I immediately sent back the device. However, after two weeks of cancellation, I received bills asking me to pay a monthly fee plus one-time charge. T-Mobile is now on my blacklist.

The worst company ever. They’ve blocked everything without reason. It’s says I haven’t paid the bill even though I paid on time and never missed any. The internet is not working most of the time. It’s scam

There was a 30 day trial period for switching to t-mobile.  If you switched from another carrier to t-mobile and switched back within 30 days they would reimburse you 150.00.  We tied tmobile and were unsatisfied with their coverage and service.  we switched back to verizon witinin the 30 days of starting tmobile and called tmoble customer service and started a claim for the 150.00.  The customer service rep stated that we were within the 30 days and expect a check mailed to address.  If we did not get the check wining 30 days call back.  Today 10/10 i called customer service at tmobile and stated never received money they said that the claim was denied because i did not re-claim my 150 with in 30 days of initial claim.

The customer service has been pleasant. However, their 5g coverage is not as they say. The data on my phone lags in a lot of places. I switched from AT&T, got home and my IPAD had trouble opening a simple google search. I went to the store where I purchased a plan and they pulled up a map showing 5g coverage. The bars on my device say otherwise. Cost me over 600 dollars to get out of the contract, that provided disruptive service.

If I could leave a zero I would.  I signed up for the internet service, which didn't work well in my home.  I cancelled it literally days later.  The process to cancel was easy and the person who helped me was great.  The following month I get a bill for $2.50.  I go online to try to pay the bill and am unable to get into my account since it is closed.  I attempt to pay as a guest, no luck.  I open a chat for help.  They tell me that I need to call to pay.  So, the next day, I call to make the payment.  Of course t-mobile uses a call center somewhere outside of America and the person I speak with speaks broken English, so explaining the situation and understanding him is not ideal.  He tells me I need to go onto the app to create a pin in order to verify who I am, so that I can make a payment.  We go back and forth and I finally ask to speak to a manager, which he refuses to transfer me to.  So even though I have my account number and am able to verify any information they need me to, they are unwilling to let me make a payment without a pin.  Which is interesting since I didn't need a pin to actually create an account with new service.  Makes perfect sense.  So now, I will have to waste more of my time to go into the store for $2.50.  Thanks T-mobile for making it so difficult to simply pay a bill.

I tried T-Mobile under the assurance that it would work in my area.  It did not, and I was unable to make or receive phone calls or Internet.  I returned the modem to the local T-Mobile store.  They still charged me a bit over $40.

I've been experiencing some ongoing issues with my phone, and after contacting T-Mobile, I was able to get a replacement device. However, the replacement turned out to be defective as well. I reached out to T-Mobile again, and they provided another replacement, which also had issues. Frustrated with the situation, I decided to file a complaint with the Better Business Bureau (BBB) and the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to seek assistance in resolving the matter and obtaining a fully functional phone.

After filing the complaint, I was contacted by a T-Mobile representative named George, who informed me that the only option available was to provide me with a fifth refurbished phone. When I expressed my concerns and asked why I couldn't receive a new phone given the recurring problems, he mentioned that they didn't have the specific model I had in brand new condition. He also suggested that the only alternative would be for me to purchase a new phone.

As a long-standing business customer of T-Mobile for the past eight years, I'm disappointed with the treatment I've received and the inability to resolve this issue in a satisfactory manner.

Thier customer service is a joke , these people don't know thier ass from a hole in the ground .  USELESS!!!  to the core just hand them your money that's all they're good for. They have no control over changing passwords it's all up to the app developer and that person has awful reviews. But the reps are carrying on like they know what to do and the information they give is all wrong and different every time  Do these ppl even really work there?  Or is this another BS move on americans??

I am receiving invoices on my email address, even though I never signed up for an account and do not have any relation with T-mobile. It is not possible to log in to T-mobile with the email address, or reset the password to enable login. T-mobile customer service says they will not help at all.

This company has gotten to be the most scandalous company I have ever seen they are liars and they are thieves and beware beware people don't do not do business with them unless it's prepaid and then even then I would be cautious!

SCAM ARTISTS AND CON ARTISTS!! I have been using tmobile home internet in our camper since 2023. I went with this option because I was told by multiple employees including the store manager that I could take it on the road to use in multiple locations as I live in my camper. For $55 a month I couldn't complain. I have been using this for over a year with NO issues and now am being told I need to pay 2.9 times more ($160 on autopay) for their new "away plan" for the EXACT same service I have been using. The home internet will now be geo locked to one location so you can't take it anywhere. The rich have to stay rich and they will find ways to make more money for providing the same service

All I have to say is SCAM ALERT. 
Don’t trust them they will SCAM you. 
Just read all their reviews. Everyone is saying they’ve been scammed and my wife and I have experienced ourselves. I would highly recommend leaving them and going to ATT. Same plan better service and at least you know you won’t be scammed

T-Mobile is not allowed to take and sell your personal information as a telephone service provider.  So T-Mobile invented T-Mobile Tuesdays (a “gift” lottery) to enable them to take and sell your personal data, and they created various means to force/trick you into signing up whether or not you want to.

The trap that I fell into (and plainly am not the first) is that if one signs up for T-Mobile Home Internet service, the only way to configure the T-Mobile router/modem in one’s home (e.g., to choose a name and password for your WiFi) is to use a specific T-Mobile app on one’s phone, and one cannot sign into the T-Mobile app without signing up for T-Mobile Tuesdays.  The T-Mobile Tuesdays terms and conditions are a near complete surrender of all of your rights, specifically including the rights to your personal data including your location data.

So, T-Mobile is selling an over-the-air carrier service (presumably under an FCC tariff) that is crippled at the user end unless the user agrees to an unbelievably onerous set of terms and conditions that, among other things, allows the disclosure of your personal information.

If one thinks that I am kidding about how onerous the terms and conditions are, here are a few quotes (available online on the T-Mobile Tuesdays T&Cs page):

“… this contract includes indemnities to the Sponsor from you, a limitation of your rights and remedies, binding arbitration of claims, waiver of class action claims and waiver to the right to trial by jury.”  For those not familiar, “indemnities” is where you agree to be responsible for the problems of T-Mobile Tuesdays – you have to take care of the billion-dollar corporation.

“By entering the Sweepstakes, Participants unconditionally: […]  b. grant (and agree to confirm that grant in writing) Sponsor, the Prize Partner and each of their respective designees to use Prize Winner’s name, photograph, likeness, voice, biographical information, statements and address (city and state) for advertising and/or publicity purposes worldwide and in all forms of media now known or hereafter developed (including, but not limited to publishing the Prize Winner’s name on Sponsor’s and the Prize Partner’s Instagram and/or Facebook pages), in perpetuity, without further compensation, except in Tennessee and where prohibited by law; and c. agree that Participant’s wireless service provider may release any Participant’s phone number(s) to the Sponsor or its designees for use in contacting Participant and determining Participant’s eligibility to receive the prize.”

“To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, by entering, each Participant (and guest, if applicable) and each of their agents, assignees, executors and successors agree to indemnify, defend, hold harmless and fully release and forever discharge each of the Released Parties and their respective shareholders from and against any and all liabilities, costs, claims, alleged claims, demands, duties, rights, agreements, promises, damages, injuries, losses, expenses, liens and debt actions of any kind, including, without limitation, attorneys’ fees, whether now known or unknown, unforeseen or foreseen, arising out of or in any way connected with, directly or indirectly, participation in the Sweepstakes, including without limitation, personal injury or death, loss of enjoyment, or other harm or loss of any nature whatsoever caused or contributed to by or arising out of participation in this Sweepstakes or resulting from acceptance, possession, quality, use or misuse of a prize. The Released Parties are not responsible for any additional expenses, omissions or delays resulting from any acts of any government or authority.”  Yep, you get to the pay the attorney fees for these big corporations to defend themselves against any wrongdoing, including a wrong done to you!

But hey, at least then you can pick a WiFi name and password for your T-Mobile Home internet.  Congratulations!

T-Mobile is dishonest and disgusting. My problems are twofold. Since the beginning on 2024, I had three warranty exchanges of my Samsung S22 handset. With each warranty exchange, T-Mobile sent a LOCKED device although my initial phone was unlocked. Calling customer service is useless as there is no resolution despite the seemingly understanding attitude from representatives who are not allowed to deviate from the script. Among the various answers for receipt of a locked phone were the exchanged phone must be on the account 90 days. Parenthetically, I paid a fee on each exchange although I was replacing a defective phone T-Mobile sent me less than one month prior.  The second issue that occurred twice involves T-Mobile stating they did not receive the damaged phone and then billing a $1500 no-return fee. In each instance, T-Mobile eventually found the phone the returned - one month later - stating the wrong IMEI was keyed in when received at their warehouse. Twice?? It seems T-Mobile has internal theft problem and would rather pass the expense onto the consumer rather than clean their house.

They  charge too much for this sh** box.  It 's always stuck on the low setting  and you have to unplug it every time to get  my television to work . I cant watch golf now. Switc h to get your club on . Bossman Husk.

T Mobile has changed in the three plus years since we joined.  When we signed up, we opted for the then highest rate plan. Now that plan is not only a "legacy" plan, we receive fewer considerations for deals than someone with their bottom most rate plan, which is still a lower price than we pay.  

T mobile basically changed nothing but the name and wants to charge my family more per moth for the privilege of signing up for that.  

When you call customer service, they will make a deal with you, but tell you that the "back office " needs to approve it.  Then they will wait two weeks and finally call you to tell you that the "back office" has declined the deal and try to force you into the more costly new plans at no benefit to you. 

This is the essence of dishonest negotiations. 

Meanwhile, ATT is offering essentially the same service, at a lower price, and will still extend customers the trade in deals.  I think that it is only a matter of time before we change back to ATT.

I’ve been dealing with T-Mobile for months, and it has been an absolute nightmare. In June 2024, I ordered a 5G internet plan and a Go 5G phone plan. Despite never receiving the order, I’ve been charged every month. I've made countless calls and sent emails, but there's been no resolution. Every time I speak with a customer service agent, I'm told a different story, full of empty promises and lies. It’s beyond frustrating.

What’s worse is that I’ve been receiving constant threats that the charges will be sent to collections, despite their reassurances that it would be corrected. It's been over three months, and I still haven’t received a refund for the order that was never delivered. This company is acting more like a scam than a reliable service provider. It's unbelievable how such a large company can operate with this level of dishonesty and incompetence.

T-Mobile, you need to do better. I feel like I’ve been duped, and no customer should have to go through this! Beware of their false promises and lack of accountability

I was previously a T-Mobile customer for years. They kept increasing the bill, and the customer service continued to be lower. The phone prices and incentives were less and less. I got really tired of paying full price for a new phone every two years and not getting a discount or any kind of incentive for my kiddos phone line. After I switched my number to a new service, they sent me a bogus charge of $103.46, claiming "equipment not returned." What equipment? I had my phone fully paid off and I didn't have any other equipment? I tried to call customer service and they said they couldn't help me without some six digit pin. I had no idea what it was because I never use a pin when I call they verify me through a text message. When I escalated the call to a supervisor, the supervisor basically said they couldn't help me either and I had to go into a T-Mobile store with my ID to be verified. I understand having security measures but when you're not even a customer anymore and you can't even have a manager tell you what the charge is for over the phone and that's the only information you're requesting that is a broken system. I offered to give my drivers license number my Social Security number my email address, have an email verification, and five other solutions but they refused to do anything without some obscure pin I don't even remember making when I first signed up.. 
Needless to say, I will never use this service again and I don't recommend anyone else comes here unless you want to overpay for your phones and not be able to be helped with your bill. 
I say this as somebody who works in customer support, and knows how systems can be created to truly help people.

I gave 1 star because it was the only way I would be able to write this review they careless about your situation at all it's like my phone was hacked I just kept getting an echo or when I dial a number I would get nothing but the phone would hang up on its on this went on for 4 day technician suppose to call back didn't happen 09.29/2024 to present this happen billing cycles change when their ready to change them if your out of work and can't pay your bill T mobile could care less even though their is a past record of you paying the bill 3 days later they still got their payment so no I would not recommend T mobile to no one un reliable they lie allot say what they think you want to hear

I am in Asheville, NC.  We just got hit by Hurricane Helene.  Our town looks like a warzone. We have no cell service, no internet, nothing. My complaint is this, we can get 5G because Elon Musk sent Starlinks above our town. My phone shows 5G, but I still can't look at anything.  My other family members that have Verizon & AT&T have use of their phones.  We are all sitting in the same room.  The 4 of us in the house that have Tmobile, can't do anything on our phones.  I'm going to be switching to AT&T.  Hopefully this will post.  Every now and then I am able to get a message out!!  I was happy with Tmobile until this happened.  Right above my house in Tractor Supply parking lot, Tmobile has a mini tower set up.  I can go there and sit right beside it and I still can't do anything with my phone.  It says 5G, but I can't do a thing with mine.  Also, my family that has Verizon & AT&T can call out and receive phone calls but not us with Tmobile.  Why is that Tmobile???  This has been going on since 9/28/2024.

I bought a screen protector “for life” for $50. meaning - they said - if mine cracks I just stop by the store and get a brand new one for free. 
well mine cracked and I went to the store and they said they have no records of me buying it but if I want I should purchase it again so I can have it. 
so basically they’re telling me to pay twice for the same service. I went to different T-Mobile stores and they say the same thing. 
ABSOLUTE SCAM. DO NOT recommend.

I was scam with T-Mobil and I was a customer since the call voice stream  the total amount was 691.00 they said I had contact and everyone knows they no longer do contacts. I left them because I went to their Georgia Ave location and someone hack into my and from this happened it was down hill for me. Please beware of t-mobile. This why I changed my services.

my phone is flooded with daily text messages asking
for donations to Trump campaign, my opinions about
Republican candidates, surveys and messages from
candidates. Reporting text as junk and delete doesn’t help because random numbers continue. 
Only eats up my iOS memory.

Had 3 online support people straight up lie about correcting an issue.  They just told me what they thought I wanted to hear.  One actually looked legit, had email exchanges, ect.  Had 24 hours to sign a digital document but it was cancelled about 10 hours, never giving me a chance to even see the email and sign.  Then the last one told me to watch my inbox for a confirmation which never came.  They intentionally lied, and I would advise anyone to stay far away from this unethical place which cuts your unlimited Internet to unusable speeds worse than 1997 dial up.  Now I am done with them and want to spread the word about this terrible place.  Yes I have screenshots, emails, confirmation numbers to T-Mobile manipulative games.  They wasted my time, and never had decent intentions of resolution.  They are awful, they lie to customers and it's time to tell the world about T-Mobiles ethics (they have none), because they do not need to be in business any longer due to these practices.  They have had class action lawsuits about this in the past, never learned the lesson.

fri pm switched to t-mobile, clerk said he could transfer my contacts and pictures,  did not happen.  went back mon to have them try again.  young female clerk could not make the transfer, or answer other questions on the cat s22. all she could say was she hates this phone. switched back to my other carrier.  lost $41.90 for nothing.

My experience with T-Mobile has been nothing short of a nightmare. I own a fully paid-off iPhone 14 Pro Max, and after renewing my business with T-Mobile by getting an iPhone 15 on credit, I reached out to unlock my old phone so I could send it to my brother, who is serving in the U.S. Army overseas.

Initially, they assured me that unlocking the phone would be easy. However, after 10 days of waiting, they suddenly claimed I was 36 hours late on my bill and pressured me to pay. Despite the fact that the iPhone 14 is no longer tied to my account, I paid the bill, only to receive even more excuses for why they couldn’t unlock it.

The absurdity is that this paid-off phone is free to be activated by any random person wanting to sign up for a T-Mobile line, even a stranger or my ex-wife. I even called pretending to be someone else, provided them with the IMEI number, and they were ready to sign me up with no questions asked—as long as it was with them! Ironically, they claim they can't unlock the phone.

To add insult to injury, my brother was ready to sign up with T-Mobile but needed the phone unlocked to use a local SIM while deployed. Instead of facilitating his needs, T-Mobile’s ridiculous policies have forced them to lose a military servicemember’s business. They’ve also lost mine; as soon as I finish paying off the new phone I bought from them, I’ll be moving on.

If you’re thinking about choosing T-Mobile, do yourself a favor and look elsewhere. You won’t be able to sell or gift your old phones; they’ll just become expensive trash. Instead of upholding their legal obligations, T-Mobile drags their feet and makes excuses. They’re full of it, and I can’t recommend them to anyone seeking reliable and ethical service!

Honestly,  had a great experience up to 9/24-9/26.

I have recently had lost my job and had a temporary part time job and had made a payment arragement that I really thought I could make it work.   

When I realized I would not be able to pay I called,  spoke to a rep.  All she could do was take my payment off so that checking account wouldnt overdraft. This was 9/22. I was told to call back on 9/24 after suspension to see what I could do. 

Chat on 9/24, the rep has no notes (I guess they don't write those on accounts anymore) so I have to repeat the whole process again.  They tell me there's nothing they can do. Even when I try to set up a payment for the 27th (I had a very small amount coming in). Nothing they can do. 

9/25 my van gets a radiator leak and hurricane Helene forms. I'm using chat,  had to go through the process all over,  get nothing but tolf to call the 1888 number because the hurricane might change that.   I call and start the process AGAIN.   Nope,  can't help me.   I guess knowing a customer out there who might not be able to contact loved ones in a deadly weather emergency is A OK morally. 

At this point,  I'm just curious if my phones are paid off,  because this isn't ok. Im suspended at this point, so I'm using my works wifi to use the chat feature.  Rep 5, after explaining the entire process with updates has to START THE ENTIRE PROCESS OVER.   Gets me to sign a paper to get approved for something (i think just to allow me to pay the entire bill on oct 11).  Talks me they will update me, but it'll take a few hours.   This was 12pm.  By 4pm still hadn't heard anything,  so started a chat with a rep 6 who HAS TO START THE PROCESS OVER and informs me that my letter was rejected (wasn't informed at all) and after a pissed off conversation,  I'm approved to pay 50$ on Friday, the suspension free is waived (60$) and i could make an arrangement for the rest. 

Final day 9/26:  I get paid early,  i start up a chat to pay the 50$ that I was promised abs told to pay. 

AND I HAVE TO START THE PROCESS OVER. 

I now am expected to pay the past due amount (not the 50$). And "chat" rep says they're "limited" in what they can do.   I literally show her screenshots of what was promised. 

Now they tell me to wait 24 hours for approval.  I cussed her out.   

I end up calling the 611 number and start the process for the 8th time.   I no longer have to 50$, i no longer have the 60$ service fee taken off and the "computer" can't even help me with the arragement (the dates aren't what I said I can do).

Is some of this my fault? ABSOLUTELY.

My issue is the lack of detail note taking that assists with helping reps deal with customers who have been speaking to several people. 

My issue is the lack of transparency when one rep says something can be done and it can't be done by another one. 

My issue is the lack of flexibility that reps used to have to assist customers whose lives just got slammed with too much stuff at once. 

Asking your customers to beg friends and family for money is not ok.  That customer may not have a friend or family.   There are people without support systems. 

Leaving your reps as shark bait for upset customers is a really crappy thing to do to employees.

An inflexible computer system that doesn't leave any places for reps to assist with customer service leads to irate customers. 

After this experience I felt unseen,  not listened to,  and unimportant.

That's why I'm leaving.

Stay away from this company!! their service is horrible, they lie and are untrustworthy! after me and my wife paid our devices off (with account up to date) and being a customer over a year, they would not unlock our devices after couple attempts and speaking to different customer service (after they initially said they would but needed to wait first was 48 hr then 72 hr then more excuses after that), there was always an excuse. and giving us the run around about this, then asking why we need our phones unlocked? all to keep stealing from you! all this does is frustrate the customer and making them want to leave even more! they need to do better, but I am done dealing with them! do yourself a favor and look elsewhere.
Had problem with phone got no help they told me I had to go to repair shop I could not get hardly any call's   I will not recommend them to anybody.went to them many times never got help

Some how it is hard to say that is there a worse cell phone provider than Straight Talk. T-Mobile has made the bottom of the list of all cell service providers. Reps make many excuses and try to make the company rules work for them and thrown the customer under the bus. Corporate stores vs authorized dealers are so different that they do not align with corporate. The sales rep at Walmart was a complete liar and tells you what she wants to gain a sale. I have been dealing with these none working phones for 4 months. Billing issues for 3 months of over charges, cause the Walmart rep did not set it up correctly, Then phone reps promising that it has been fixed. Three months later still automatic drafts with over charges. So I am out of town and T-Mobile overcharged me and I was not able to get a hotel room for the night ( I was out on business), so my wife and I had to sleep at the grocery store parking lot. I address the concern with T-Mobile and they did not care. My phones from day one had not worked properly since day one. I called and had many promises to repair, call backs and neither had been done. The last rep/supervisor over looked that I said day one and said that I had 30 days to take the phones back but placed it on the authorized dealer when I had been calling in. Everything there after then revolved against the 30 day return policy. Therefore no resolution, tried escalating and he says he is as high as I can escalate other than writing a letter my mail. So how does it take 4 months with no resolution from a company like T-Mobile. We made the move to get away from Straight Talk bad customer service and T-Mobile ended up being the worst! So my recommendation is do not buy from an authorized dealer, they have the bottom end of the products and they do not align to T-Mobile. If there is no stock do not get into the contract as it will mess everything up. Read everything cause T-Mobile does not  care about you once they think they have you locked in. I have asked for escalation or call from corporate and was told nobody will call us back. So it really looks like I am searching another carrier.

Business account with Sprint & then T-Mobile for over 15 years. Staff member lost phone, called to replace & spent an hour with someone who obviously didn't know what they were doing. I had to pay taxes with a credit card ($95), okay fine & staff should get the phone next day or two. Received 2 emails from T-Mobile days later confirming the 2 orders & wrong shipping address on both. Noticed too they charged my card twice for the $95. Called the next day trying to get them to reverse the double charge & cancel the duplicated order. They did not help & ended up getting disconnected. Called back & found out BOTH phones were shipping to my office in another state from where the phone was supposed to go. They ended up having to place an entire new order & this time I paid $48 in taxes??? Well 2 phones showed up at the office this morning & we refused shipment as we were told to...now let's see how long it takes to get the account straightened up. By the way the reason they checked for the returns was "buyers remose", I objected & said hope there's a box to check for T-Mobile mess up. Will definitely be looking for another provider.
I recently spoke to a CS Rep Melisa and she was professional. She handled my concerns and questions and helped me make an informed decision. Very pleasant and glad I got her when I called support.

I transferred devices from one account to another in 2023. Multiple times I contact t mobile because the devices were not transferred. I was told they would take care of it. They never did. They sent me to collections for those devices, even after they verified I contacted them about the issue. I spoke with the store manager and other store employee's with no resolution. I have been trying to get this taken out of collections and off my credit due to their error and they refuse. I have no problem paying off the devices, I just want it off my credit and the payment plan for the devices honored. The only valid reason I can come up with is the fact a family member who worked there was sexually harassed, was physically assaulted, and received multiple death threats from staff at the Yreka, Ca store, so as a punishment to my family for having the manager and other 2 employee's involved terminated for this behavior.

I've been a T-Mobile customer for many years, most of which have been excellent. Unfortunately, I had to choose a different service recently, but T-Mobile customer service was excellent even when I was switching carriers. I'll be back for sure when circumstances permit!

T-mobile deserves zero stars. This is a very expensive service that cannot back up the high prices with not even mediocre service. I consistently had a single bar, the spinning wheel of death, a minimum of 40 minutes wait time and up to 4.5 hours on hold with customer service. The promise to buy your phone is ONLY on paper. You actually get nothing for a trade in. I was also promised $150 in Sam’s club gift cards. I never saw them. I had to pay $700 to purchase my phone so I could get a better carrier for more than 1/2 the cost and far superior service. AVOID TMOBILE!!!!

Have a Business account with T-Mobile and have had excellent payment history for over a year. Nothing financed on my account. Was trading in me and my partners phone for a $1,000 credit per phone. New phones cost $1,200. They still want a $450 Down payment per phone even though that would make it $500 more then the total purchase and they will “ Pay it back “ over 24 months in bill credits. So I’m giving them a $500 interest free loan that they take 2 Years to pay back? Probably taking my business to another carrier. This is ridiculous and makes no mathematical sense. Think 2ce before switching to t-mobile.

I am beyond frustrated with T-Mobile right now. I have been a loyal customer for nearly 22 years. In fact, T-Mobile is the only cell phone provider I have ever been with, and I have sung your praises since I was 16 years old. For the first time, I am so upset over your processes and failed promises, I am ready to take my 5+ lines of service elsewhere. Not only am I considering leaving T-Mobile, but I am prepared to tell every and any one who will listen about my horrendous experience. While that may not mean much to you, word of mouth travels quickly - especially if I go to the media, (which they may or may not care to cover, but I will literally not stop until I'm heard).  

I have been trying to access my OWN PERSONAL phone records from November of 2022. After much run-around from representatives stating it wasn't possible, I finally spoke with someone from one of your physical locations who not only informed me it is possible, but he also told me how to get them. I wish I could remember his name because I was very grateful for his willingness to help. Per his instructions, I called, "611," and asked for a reprint. Sure enough, that seemed to be all I needed at the time. I was assured I would receive my reprint in the mail within 10 business days. 

10 business days came and went, and I never received my statement. I called again, and a representative by the name of Angel, was very empathetic. He ordered a 2nd reprint for me, and he even offered to follow up with me within a week to ensure I received them. The week went by, and Angel called as he promised (super grateful for that), but I still hadn't received my reprint. He then told me he would request a manager to upload them and email them to me. He said it might take 24 hrs as they were really busy. 

I gave them 48 hours to get the job done, and yet nothing happened AGAIN. I called "611" that evening, and I reached a different representative who said they would send another request for management to email my records to me. She said she would call me back the following day at 10:00 AM while a manager was present to ensure it happened. Guess who never received a follow up call...

That evening, after work, I called into the "611" line, and I explained the situation once more. I was assured yet another empty promise that I would have my November 2022 statement in my email within 24 hours. Do I have my phone records yet? No. No, I do not. 
Never, in my wildest dreams, did I ever think that it would be so difficult to request my own personal phone records. DO BETTER, T-Mobile; do better!

I switch to t-mobile 4 phone line and 2 warless internet. the worse customer service they never respond, well yes but only if you a new customer after that no respond I was waiting on hold almost and hour in to different times big mistake to switch to T-mobile

I have had t mobile for over 2 years now because I was stuck in a plan with my sister. Over the last year I've realized how terrible they're service really is. I can't even make calls at my own house without the WIFI. On top of that, every time I try to contact customer service It's super sketchy and I never actually get solutions. I am quitting today because I haven't had service for over a week and I've talked to three different people about the situation all gave me the same resolution. It would work for about an hour and then my service would be blocked again. I have never been as upset at any company as much as I am with T Mobile. All they do is take your money and cause issues. You would have better luck buying minutes than with this company. Absolutely horrible.
After 2 days sim card doesn't work, the customer service is useless, it, a a falraud, don't buy it!

I paid for insurance on my two phone lines. Been with the company 32 years. Cracked my screen, went to the T mobile store on Ann Arbor rd in Plymouth  mi, and was refused any kind of assistance or re -imbursment for my damaged phone. The screen repair was supposed to be covered with a co-pay. They said no, gave me a card for a generic repair place 20 minutes away. 300 dollars later I had a new screen. Don't get suckered into paying for something that they will not honor. Don't get the protection plan. I'm sorry I did.

They would get a 0 if I could. We shut service down for many of the same reasons you see here. We received a bill -$45.00 so the owed me, but instead continued to charge my card. When contacted they said we owed them some $125.00 then sent me to a collection agency when I stopped payment on my card. Now I get another bill -$45.00 yet won't give it back.

Response to request of contact

Response to request of contact.

The team member was very professional and understanding.
I apologize for my demeanor.

T-Mobile wi fi service is a disgrace !!

MORE THAN ONCE MY DEVICES HAVE BEEN DISCONNECTED FROM THE INTERNET.

I HAVE SPENT HOURS ATTEMPTING TO RESOLVE THESE ISSUES.

USUALLY ON THE VERGE OF CONTACTING A TECHINICIAN OR REPAIR PERSON.

I RECENTLY LOOKED AT THE T-MOBILE TRANSMITTER AND DISCOVERED THAT THE DEVICES WERE NOT ACTIVE ON THE TRANSMITTER.

APPARENTLY, THE TRANSMITTER DISCONNECTS DEVICES RANDOMLY.

FURTHER, WHEN WATCHING A STREAMING SERVICE THE SIGNAL IS WEAK.

AFTER REBOOTING THE SIGNAL GOES TO EXCELLENT FOR A FEW MINUTES THEN RETURNS TO FAIR.

THE PROGRAM/SPORTS EVENT IS CONSTANTLY INTERUPTED AS THE SIGNAL ALTERNATES.

NOT A PLEASANT EXPERIENCE.

I WILL START EVALUATING MY INTERNET PROVIDER OPTIONS TODAY. ( 9-12-2024 )

FURTHER, MY DECEASED WIFE`S ACCOUNT WAS CLOSED ABRUPTLY RESULTING IN THE LOSS OF THREE PHONE NUMBERS, CONTACT DATA, ETC.

T-MOBILE IS NOT A GOOD SERVICE PROVIDER !!!!

Date of experience: September 01, 2024

text: If you are a tourist avoid the prepaid t-mobile. The coverage was so poor on the west coast that after a week of use I went back to a store to find out what was wrong. It was the worst wifi prepaid I have experienced in 11 countries. I was told it was unlimited data for US$65 a month, but it seems the prepaid speed is slowed when t-mobile plan users are on the network (they get priority), and it is not unlimited. At 50G you get a message to say you still have unlimited data, but it slows to unusable speeds. Misleading sales and terr

I was talked into signing the 5G next plan ($180/mo) to get 2 new phones and and several rebates. 
It turns out $300 of that rebate was no long valid at the time T-Mobile offered me the rebate, and their customer service rep even applied the rebates for me. After 4 months, I check the status of the rebates and they were denied since, again those rebates were dated. 
T-Mobile is only willing to make up 50% of my loss on rebate. 

I cannot trust their sales person or the customer service rep that applied the rebates for me. I will be switching services.

T-Mobile is terrible customer service is garbage the actual service is not what they say it is I can have 5G 3 bars and it still slow internet customer service is completely unhelpful they don't care about helping you at all go in to the store and its like they don't even know what they are talking about they will lie to you about everything

Awful company! I haven't used their services for 2 months and they still requested me to pay $200. When I called customer service, I asked multiple times if I would be charged more and was told no. Guess what? I received yet another bill with $24.95 to pay. Called customer service again and they requested PIN (which I paid $20 for and don't have anymore since my account has been cancelled). For not having this pin the charges can't be removed. Online help refused to help me too. Worst mobile company ever. Switching to Mint and will never look back.

They can do whatever they want and get away with it because they have a monopoly on cell towers. That's also why they aren't incentivized to provide quality service to their customer. It's a horrible company and anytime that you have to contact them for any issue, it's like torture. Longest phone calls ever, most inept service ever and everything is ridiculously expensive.

I switched to T Mobile in April, 2024. During the switch, the rep offered me a “free” tablet to accompany the tablet line for my granddaughter. Unfortunately, neither the phones nor the tablet ever had service at my house. After over 2 months of no service at my house, I cancelled and switched back to Verizon. Of course, upon cancellation, the tablet was suddenly no longer free and not returnable. So, I paid the balance and was assured by the rep that it would be unlocked and available to use on any other network. I took him at his word but did not attempt to use the tablet until the end of August. That is when I discovered that it was still locked to T Mobile. I have spoken to MANY different techs and even went back to the original store in an effort to get the thing unlocked. Now, T Mobile is telling me that it is unlocked (it’s not) and refuses to help anymore. I spent several hundred dollars on a device that I couldn’t use (it’s still locked because it was only on their network for several days and was on WI-FI the rest of the time) and still can’t use because I couldn’t use it on their network due to having no service at my house. STAY AWAY FROM THIS SCAM!!

I am disappointed in T-Mobile now. They charge $10 to pay a bill AND you have to agree to all the terms & conditions before you can use the app. So I am deleting the app since I don't agree to 3rd parties having my info. I will be switching from T-Mobile elsewhere. They also use 5G that's harmful to our bodies and children's bodies!
I’m with them

Bought a plan for my elderly dad. It never even worked! I tried to cancel but the only way to do it was to travel to the nursing home where my dad was and have a pin sent. Even when calling on the phone! They accept nothing but a pin sent to the phone. They sent me to collections when you can clearly see the phone had never been used. AWFUL customer service

I went with T-Mobile over 20 years ago, back when their signal was crappy at best and everybody had Verizon. I loyally hung in there, now they have grown and have a good signal.  My issue is, after all these years, I CAN NOT get a free phone if my life depended on it. I have tried MANY times, spent hours on the phone with them. I see all these free phone offers for new customers, but not for me. I'd have to change my plan to a much more expensive one AND trade my phones, ( I have 4 lines). Amazingly none of my phones qualify for trade either. (go figure, they're old). I have the plan I can afford and will not change my plan to get free phones. I believe T-Mobiles goal is to get me to switch to a expensive plan so I can get a new phone, they are playing dirty. I am looking at other carriers now, they may be more but they'll give you a new phone, so it evens out. 24 years with them and that's my reward??? Shame on them!
Thanks for NOTHING!!
Go somewhere else for your service, because once you're their customer they treat you like crap and take you for granted.

A Deeply Disappointing Experience with T-Mobile’s Services.
I had specifically requested to block both roaming and international calls on my account due to previous concerns about T-Mobile’s practices. After confirming multiple times with customer support that these services were blocked, I still received charges for roaming. When I called to resolve the issue, I spent an agonizing 47 minutes with a representative who ultimately informed me that the block hadn't been applied correctly to individual accounts. This level of negligence is completely unacceptable.

T-Mobile’s support and service have proven to be unreliable and misleading. As a customer, I feel scammed by their inability to deliver on basic requests. This is by far the worst customer service experience I have encountered.

I am an 11 year customer with multiple lines on my account. My wife and daughter upgraded their phones t-mobile sent 2 iPhone Pro phones when we had ordered the iPhone Pro Max. Upon contacting you, you sent a return label and we shipped the unopened boxes back to t-mobile. We were told once the shipped phones are received by the warehouse the new phones will be shipped out. The tracking number shows that you (the warehouse) received the iPhone Pro phones, shortly after we received the iPhone Pro Max phones. My account has still not been updated to show that the phones have been received in the warehouse.

I’ve called T-Mobile customer service five times with each representative, claiming that they’re going to see this through and call me back. So far none of the reps or supervisors have resolved this issue. My last call I’ve escalated to second-level management promising to get me video surveillance showing when UPS dropped off the phones, and paper trail in the wearhouse to track what happened with the package and where it is now.. I received an email stating that they can’t help me with this matter and the phones can’t be found, therefore no refund will be issued.

I’ve read through multiple threads on the internet, this appears to be an ongoing problem with this multi billion dollar company scamming and taking advantage of their customers. I am currently being charged over $2000.00 for Phones that I sent back to them and are not in my possession.

T-Mobile is giving me many conflicting answers, some reps have said the phones have been received and it’s has not been scanned in, some are telling me the boxes were empty although they confirmed the weight of the packages indicate the phones would be in the box, and some reps are telling me that the package is nowhere to be found. We went to UPS and they confirmed delivery has been made. I have filed complaints with FCC, Department of Consumer Affairs and have reached out to multiple media agencies to get this issue on the media. I need resaloution but no one in your company seems to care.
Just a home WiFi and phone cellular data like a snail never run anything can’t do anything with it everything are bad

I had a T-Mobile money account, just to lower by bill.,lil throw card. Put 500.00 on the card for gas. Fast food. Entertainment. Never used the card. Forgot about it. I happen to check my spam from stuff piled up. I seen I had the account. Somehow lost the card along the way. Chargers were obviously made miles away from me. All online. Account was comprised during the data breach. Froze my account and no refund. Customer service is rude as pucks

T-mobile upset me a lot and I would normally give them a one star but on the other side, I must say that they have sometimes outstanding people that are genuinely concerned about your problem and they help you, understand the issue and so extremly professional like the one I spoke today. His name is Axel. He was the best so far. Because of him, I will give T-Mobile a 5*. That’s how good he was! I will give them a 5*.

I needed help with my home internet, and the app wasn't working, so I was told to go to a store.  I went to store number 5GSP in Stow, Ohio.  The personnel there were friendly, knowledgeable, and helpful.  I was able to obtain quick service.  Good job!

So a representative changed my due date with out my authorization and caused a short bill to be generated for 79 dollars, from this they added it to my normal bill of 200 dollars and are now saying I owe $279. I spoke with CJ a manager at tmobile customer service and he said sorry that happened even though we did this without your permission we aren't able to do anything or waive the mistaken bill. That is crazy they won't even fix a mistake they caused.

The worst phone company I ever been they over charge me more than 500 for two month and when I left them, with out tell me they send me to collection with out tell me anything Saying that I have 63 dollars for the last month bill which is lie. My bill uses to be 120 for 2 phone but in two month they over charge me and mess-up my credit Run away from this thief company
T mobile is robbing seniors. They upped me 15. Let's all move over to Pure Talk. T Mobile does not respect us.

My family and I have used T-Mobile for close 10 years and other than the price of the plans, the company wasn't that bad but then again what choice do you have? When my husband and I separated I chose to change phone plans to separate our phone bill. We have been trying to get T-Mobile to unlock the phone for over three weeks now and we still cannot use OUR phones with the new company. Mind you, our phones are completely paid for. I have called multiple times and spent several hours of my time arguing with customer service and nothing has helped resolve the issue. I requested to speak to a manager and they told me that they would say the same thing. It has left me no choice but to leave reviews. Our phones were $1,200 a piece and now they are paperweights. I am so done with phone companies thinking they can screw us over.

edits.text: I started using T-Mobile internet after I got tired of the service I was using overcharging me for data. Big mistake! I lose service almost everyday. Every time I am having problems with my phone I am also having problems with my internet. And to top it all off, customer service is a joke and they take forever to get a hold of. I'm about to switch the entire thing to a cable service instead.

text: If I could give negative 5 stars, I would. They are terrible. Run, run, run!!! I called to order home internet. The guy on the phone convinced me to transfer my cell coverage to T mobile. I decided to transfer my cell coverage. But, I changed my mind after reading reviews. Before I received the SIM cards for my phones, I called to cancel the phones. But...I have been charged month after month for these phones. What a total nightmare! Run, Run Run!!
From the beginning I was offered one thing and received nothing.... but a bill.... horrible customer service horrible everything and service isn't that great.....
I got a bill said I had 9.50 refund no money I call them said can't get in account without pin I don't know they got into it when I owed them CHICKEN crap BUNCH

I switched from Verizon to T-Mobile.  In general, the service has been very good, however, I've encountered two major issues that are unacceptable. 

First, when I bought an iPhone and Android, I specified that I wanted insurance on both.  When I lost the Android, I contacted T-Mobile and was told that the iPhone was covered but the Android was not.  T-Mobile would not honor the commitment the local store made re. the Android being covered.

At the time I purchased the two phones, the salesperson convinced me that I would benefit from having trackers.  I made it clear that I traveled extensively internationally, and would need to track my luggage internationally.  I've taken several trips trying to use the trackers but they did not work.  Today, T-Mobile stated that my trackers would work only domestically, and that only businesses could subscribe to the international service  So, I've been paying monthly charges for these trackers that do not work as advertised.

I never encountered this type of misrepresentation from Verizon, and am seriously considering going back to them.
We ordered two sync up devices and T-Mobile will not activate the devices without us having to travel to a store and verify who we are.  Spend the extra money and go with Verizon or AT&T.

I have been with tmobile for 12 years and I trusted the internet service, DONT GET IT, IT DOESNT WORK AND AFTER YOU CALL THEM many times trying to find the reason that its not working, changing location of the modem, changing the chip, changing the modem, etc, THEY WILL CHARGE FOR FULL SERVICE. Safe time and get internet somewhere else.

So I get a phone call August 28th schedule appointment for September 3rd 2024 6:30 a.m. no phone call no nothing I make an inquiry via text they tell me my application was denied due to location this is a classic corporate negligence they're so desperate the left hand has no idea what the right hand is doing and they could care less who they step on. I can tell you this from my experience. If this used car lot treat you like this before you even get to the interview how would you like to have your whole future depending on this scab outfit..... if you're a young technician avoid this place like the plague I'm in his trade 30 years this is a scab outfit

I had Tmobile for 10+ years and LOVED them. The international service, the upgrades, the billing. ALL of it. The only reason I switched is after I paid off my iPhone 13 someone messed something up and I could not upgrade my phone over a year. I went to AT+T huge mistake. (See my review) . I would love to go back to Tmobile if they ship my phone to Bali and pay off my ATT obligation

TMobile Home internet service is simply Horrific.  The last time I experienced internet this awful was when internet first became a thing.  The Buffering, Freezing and weak to no signal when it rains MEANS JUST GIVE UP.  There is no savings when you are paying for frustration.  No matter how many times I contacted TMobile to complain about these issues, none of their fixes, or suggestions were a remedy.  I ended up tolerating this bad service for six months, at which point I could adequately afford to change providers.  The worst part is; I cancelled my service on 9/02/24 and was told I would still be billed for the whole month of September because my bill closing date was 25 days away.  Then I was told that I would also be responsible for a final bill in October.  TMobile should have been paying me for all the times I called for support, because the service was randomly available .and at a level where it was useful to all members of the family

About a year ago I went to do a trade in for an iPhone 14 Pro. I traded in my Note 10 plus and got the trade in “promotion” of $850 and the credit would be applied monthly in the bills. They said that all was done and I got the phone. First month and credit didn’t show. So I went to back at the same store and they told me it would “take some time to show up”. OK, I wait another month and paid my bill again with no credit shown. So I went back to the store and they told me that customer service would have to investigate why. They then told me again to wait until customer service contacts them back. This time I waited 2 months and went back to the same store. They said they need more time. At this point I waited a year and I decided to investigate my self. I personally called customer service and they said they would investigate and see why the “credit” didn’t go thru. I then waited another 6 days I was told that the device was “damaged” when it arrived at the warehouse. How can it be “damaged” if in the store it was tested and seen by an associated. I don’t know if they are just trying to cover their tracks and get out of this problem but it was not handled well. I ended up giving up my almost brand new note  10 plus for free and paying full price for the iPhone.
I have been a customer for 22 years. Now, they keep on overcharging me. I am very disappointed. I will switch to a different company

I sent my iphone back with the label provided and was told they never received it.  Months later $400 was taken out of my checking.  Wow!  During this time, I was full-time caregiver for my mom who was dying.  I will never trust or use this immoral company again.  Beware!

Zero stars if permitted by Trustpilot. I had been with T-Mobile when it was a very good cellular service provider. Since the gotcha notification of the price increase a few months ago, T-Mobile service has plummeted. They are now as bad or worse than AT&T, Comcast, Zoom and the long list of corporations with dismal reputations for customer care. Long hold times to reach a T-Mobile support agent are now standard operating procedure. As I am writing this review I have been on hold for over 1 hour and 24 minutes to reach a tech support agent. This is unacceptable. No one from T-Mobile is attempting to remedy the long hold times. Anyone who selects T-Mobile as their cellular phone service provider should be examined for neurological damage.

edits.text:
What has happened to T-Mobile customer service? When I joined T-Mobile 2 years ago, T-Mobile phone support agents were understanding, helpful and competent. Since the summer of 2022, everything having to do with T-Mobile customer service has been for the worst.

I now have to routinely deal with incompetent, $2 an hour, outsourced employees from foreign countries that cannot adequately speak or understand English. In the unlikely event I receive assistance from an agent located in the United States, it's usually someone with an attitude problem.

I spend hours and hours on the phone with T-Mobile agents in order to eventually achieve results that more often should be accomplished with just a quick phone call. These numbskulls do little but waste my time.

T-Mobile is going to lose loyal customers in droves if they do not solve the company's customer support issues. Additionally, the two security breaches in less than two years will not help T-Mobile's tarnished image.

T-Mobile Tuesday is a disappointment. Free Netflix is a generous offer but Netflix is awful. And T-Mobile has recently eliminated free Pandora weekends, one of its best perks.

T-Mobile executives are making all the wrong decisions and it shows. I am seriously considering leaving T-Mobile is things do not improve soon.

text:
I don't usually leave reviews (I will try to post this on other sites as well). 

Our family totally regrets switching to T-Mobile. 

It is clear that their mission is wear you down, confuse you, and leave you feeling angry and confused.

DO NOT fall for the "Keep and Switch" promotion. It is a total scam. The website for the rebate submission doesn't work. The email address they told me to use bounced back "email full". The countless hours of phone calls and re-explaining the situation is insane. 

I feel like a total loser for getting involved with this company. 

I hope this review helps someone make a better decision than we did.

The WORST!!!!!  They lie telling you they will fix any discrepancies on your bill just to turn around and fix it.  They sneak in charges every month and they bolt face steal from their customers!!!  GO ANYWHERE ELSE

How are they able to change my phone number and my password to my T-Mobile app without consent. It is beyond me. Then want to charge me to switch it back. I have a terminal illness this was the cherry on top I'm headed to hospital.

I have been a customer for 16 years and am appalled at the lack of response and service I have experienced with T-mobile. I was due to receive a return call on Thursday 8/29 regarding an escalation or investigation on my account. When T Mobile merged with sprint I continued to be charged for a device leased with sprint on 7/21. That lease agreement was for an 18 month period. My account continued to be charged 45 dollars unbeknownst to me for a device that was paid off. In dealing with another issue I saw the charge on my account and spoke to customer care. The total amount of charges post the end of my lease is estimated in the amount of 900$. I was informed T mobile could issue me a three month credit only. I requested that the three month credit be issued immediately. In addition to my request for a full refund credit to be escalated to corporate and investigated. I was reassured I would receive a call back on Thursday from Monique regarding details of that investigation. I never agreed nor signed any agreement whatsoever nor did I receive any notification that I would be billed for a leased device post the 18 month period. No one has returned my call nor was I issued an immediate 3 month credit as promised. I am again asking this matter be escalated. T-mobile makes billions of dollars in revenue from its customers. If this issue is not resolved promptly I will be seeking consultation from an attorney to look into whether or not T mobile is stealing money from its customers and refusing to return calls or refund monies that are owed to its customers. I am asking that my complaint is further escalated and forwarded to the appropriate administration. Not a customer care representative with limited experience, capability, or scope to handle this concern. Please review the last call that was recorded and noted on my account. I am requesting a return call from someone in administration as soon as possible. I am also requesting my account is credited immediately as promised. Thank you.

I have been extremely disappointed with this service. I was told I qualified for certain phones. And just to pick them up from the store.  The store said I didn't qualify and I ended up with crappy phones that get hot. Then I get my first bill for 47.00. The second bill went to 470. 00. Then when I could not pay that they added a 200 disconnect fees on it. Despite me calling and making arrangements and being told that I would be fine. Oh did I mention that on my bill there were like 7 lines and services that I never asked for or received. Still didn't take those off or the reconnect fees or the late fees. Then they had it setup on days that I didn't get paid on. Then when I call and try and explain to them why I am unhappy they never resolve anything. I was with atnt for years. My bill was the same 75 a month. With tmobil I get 3 bills a month. And I could continue for ever. Worst service ever. They even charge u for customer service. Wow. Unbelievable.

I was going to leave my current internet and cell phone provider and start both services with T-Mobile. However, when I called my existing provider to cancel they gave me better rates, so I decided to stay with them and called T-Mobile to cancel my intended switch well before the intended switch date, so neither service was ever set up at all. So I thought all is settled. However, I received a letter from a debt collector for unpaid T-Mobile bill.  Up until then I had not even received a bill from T-Mobile – that came later, even after I called T-Mobile to complain and they told me all is good.  I called the again. I was told all is well again “since you never even started the services and we see that.”  Comes another bill. I call them again. The rep says he is going to refer this to their fraud department.  Idk what logic that makes, but that’s supposedly to try to put a stop to this madness. Some days go by and I get a letter from another debt collector. This is where we are today.  This T-Mobile in my judgment is a mad house!!! I will steer away from them like a plague. Now I have to AGAIN spend a lot of time placing more calls… or I might consult an attorney.
I call the debt collector. Told them the events above and how I then owe nothing.  He tried to tell me the service begins when I receive SIM cards even if the actual service has not been started. I told him that’s bs. Then I called T-Mobile. Explained all again to the rep. She consulted her supervisors, whoever, and upon returning said there is nothing she can do because it has been submitted to debt collector. The rep was nice and sympathized with my situation. She didn’t think I should be charged for services never rendered. I may have to get an attorney if this nonsense doesn’t stop.

I signed up for the FREE TRIAL via t mobil. I received the equipment and was able to get the price down at my current carrier. I called t mobil and was directed to return the equipment to my local store,which I did. I was directed by the agent in the store to call and cancel to save myself a bill. I called and was reassured I would get no bill amd they would back date the bill to protect me from getting billed. This was on August 15th 2024. Today, August 30 2024,I received a bill for $56!!!! I called again....Another non-e glish speaking chart reader that couldn't and wouldn't help me. Just kept repeating the same thing and trying to ask for the same info!! After 10 min I was so angry and asked for a supervisor. After another 15 mon hold I finally hung up!!! This company is bogus!!! DO NOT TRUST THEM IN ANY WAY!!!

Couldn't even use the data for 3 months. Can't make calls while on the road. Luckily I have wifi at work and home.
Asked Tmobile tech for help still couldn't figure it out. 
He suggested I just upgrade to a free phone.  Ok, sounds good. But Tmobile wouldn't take the phone unless I replace the tiny crack on the screen ($120). 
Decided to switch to Verizon. Gave me three new phones with much better reliability. I guess Tmobile would rather nickel and dime their existing customers and lose a customer after 7 years. What kind of stupid business model is that?
Personel at Verizon stores always look more professional and busy compare to Tmobile stores. Hmmm

I've had the worst experience with tmobile. I canceled my internet almost a year ago. They never stopped billing me. I also had a watch I bought. Was supposed to have it's own line. When I went to far from my phone it would say I'm to far for Bluetooth. When I took it to the store they said that It does not have its own line and that the phone number was just to keep track of making payments on the devise it self. Now 2 years later they say it was supposed to have service and over charged me $15 per month. So I totally I was over charged $785. Not to mention when I told them I did not want insurance on any of my phones come to find out they charged me for years 15 dollars a month per line. I refuse to pay the bill they say is $460. They owe me $750. I said I'm willing to cancel what I owe you and get my plan corrected. Keep the extra $350. They said that they can get it so I only owe $270. Thrn said i should have known about it. They will only do $100 credit. I have been telling them for months.  Not happening. I'm done with this company.

Drove to store on Rt 83 in Round Lake Beach IL
Went to cancel my brother's phone in my family account w T-Mobile, since he passed away.

Employee at Round Lake Beach store said I should do it over phone. I explained I already spoke w someone on phone, and didn't want to do it by phone.

He continued to instruct me to do it by phone. Finally i had enough and left. Second time this location can't help me.

Drove 20 minutes to another TMobile location 
in Antioch and they were able to help me with no questions asked

Close down the store in Round Lake Beach. They never seem helpful in any way and this is a national issue with many companies and I'm not accepting it any more

Either perform customer service for the high prices or close.

I've been with sprint and t mobile for 20+ years.

Very very disgusted.

One star because you can give zero.  Switch to T-Mobile about 6 months ago after about 20 years with Verizon.  I was lied to from the beginning, their support people do not know how a cell phone works, if you want to escalate you have to WRITE to them.  They are a technology company and you have to WRITE to them?!?!? On paper?!?!?

To be the worst ever you must really try. I have over the years had Us Cellular, Verizon, Att. US Cellular was good. In June I wanted to try TMobile  as my present carrier ATT has gone to very poor service at my home due to a tower issue and Att is not going to repair. I had TMobile service for less than 12 hours. My fist issue showed me that their Customer Care is more like Customer We Don’t Care so I cancelled everything the same day as it was set up. I was told I owed zero as I had made zero calls, zero texts, and zero internet. For the 3rd month in a row a bill for $2.67 comes. 1 hour on phone STILL UNRESOLVED. Run away from this company.
